bullets.text: Q2 CR of 86.2 companywide; Personal Lines CR 86.0 (vehicle 86.2, property 83.6); Commercial Lines CR 86.8 — all well below the 96.0 profit target.
Total PIFs exceeded 37 million, with Personal Lines growing 16% YOY and adding nearly 1 million policies in Q2; personal auto PIFs up 19% YOY — fifth consecutive quarter of double-digit PIF growth.
Companywide Q2 NPW grew 12% YOY; Personal Lines NPW grew 15% YOY; personal auto NPW increased 16% driven by strong renewals and new applications (8% growth in new apps).

Severity increased modestly in Q2 but was predominantly offset by lower loss frequency and higher average earned premiums; results not materially affected by significant weather or prior-period development.

Debt-to-total capital below 18% at Q2 end; no bond maturities until 2027; Q2 investment portfolio returned 2.1% (fixed income 1.7%, equity 10.9%); investment income contributed roughly half of total comprehensive income.
Vehicle non-acquisition expense ratio decreased 0.3 points YTD through June as cost discipline continues; tariff impacts on loss costs monitored closely with multiple scenarios modeled.

Personal auto model 8.9 available in 24 states (46% of auto NPW); Snapshot 5.0 deployed in states representing 78% of personal auto NPW; commercial model 8.3 elevated in one state with 13 additional planned by year-end.
Personal property 5.0+ models elevated in 29 states (75%+ of homeowners NPW); cargo plus endorsement available in 47 states for for-hire transportation customers; CL BOP and BQX continued expansion.

Tributes paid to former CEO Glenn Renwick, who passed in May; letter emphasized culture of community involvement with paid volunteer time-off program enabling employees to expand charitable engagement.

Commercial auto industry (ex-Progressive) estimated at 111.4 CR for 2024; CIAB data shows competitors taking high single-digit rate increases, expected to make Progressive rates more competitive as industry levels rise.
